findings.evidence: `HarmonicDilation.lean:18-20` says these lemmas normalize balls by translating and rescaling; `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace/AffineMap.lean:820` defines `AffineMap.homothety`, and `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace/AffineEquiv.lean:488` provides the nonzero affine equivalence.
findings.fix: State and prove the homothety-centered versions first, e.g. for `fun y ↦ f (AffineMap.homothety a c y)` with the same `c ^ 2` factor and nonzero iff statements, then derive the current `c • y` lemmas as the center-`0` special cases.
findings.issue: `laplacian_comp_smul_right`, `harmonicAt_comp_smul_right_iff`, and `harmonicOnNhd_comp_smul_right_iff` are only the origin-centered case `x ↦ c • x`; Mathlib has `AffineMap.homothety`/`AffineEquiv.homothetyUnitsMulHom`, and the roadmap use case is normalizing arbitrary balls.

summary: The new API stops at origin-centered scalar multiplication, but the natural Mathlib-level dilation for ball normalization is affine homothety about an arbitrary center.
